description: Breath lifts 'pharoe', the upper front region of the torso and the cavity enclosed by the ribs. The same region can locate pressure or pain. 'korua' (heart) and 'phuwai' (lung) name organs within it.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips close without sealing them and push unvoiced air through the narrow opening for 'ph', then lower the jaw into open central 'a'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge and continue into stressed middle-height back rounded 'o'. Move the tongue forward and unround the lips for middle-height 'e' in a separate final syllable.

semantic_domains.physical: the upper front torso and the cavity holding the heart and lungs
